mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off portfolio rebalancing and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in declining earnings.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in over 60% of instances where the unemployment rate spikes by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation and collapsing housing demand indicate stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered episodes, or the joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a supply-constrained environment confirms successful capacity expansion and easing bottlenecks, boosting near-term corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of rising INDPRO observations, or the signal exhibits no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate directly expands aggregate corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a six-month horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Equity returns are negative in over 55% of quarters where real GDP growth exceeds trend, or the rolling correlation between GDP surprises and SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated policy rates and persistent inflation compresses real yields and equity valuation multiples, producing strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates outpace nominal earnings growth.
regime_hint: aggressive monetary tightening with sticky inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC reduction < 5) over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth in a late-cycle expansion regime sustains consumer spending and corporate cash flows, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in over 65% of triggered episodes, or the signal produces a negative information coefficient when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and consumer spending directly correlate with S&P 500 earnings growth, generating positive forward returns as consumer-driven revenue beats drive analyst upgrades and multiple expansion.
failure_condition: Equity returns are negative in more than 50% of rising RSAFS observations, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.04 across three consecutive market cycles.
